ud8.3:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud8.3:1.3:
And on that occasion the Blessed One was instructing, urging, rousing,
& encouraging the monks with Dhamma-talk concerned with unbinding.
ud8.3:1.4: The monks–receptive, attentive, focusing their entire awareness, lending ear–listened to the Dhamma.
ud8.3:2.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.3:3.2:
If there were not that unborn–unbecome–unmade–unfabricated,
there would not be the case that escape from the born–become–made–fabricated would be discerned.
ud8.3:3.3:
But precisely because there is an unborn–unbecome–unmade–unfabricated,
escape from the born–become–made–fabricated is discerned.
